data.title: Wintermute is suspected of participating in ORCA market making again, transferring nearly 10 million US dollars of tokens in a short period of time and causing the price of the currency to fall
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to the monitoring of on-chain analyst @ai_9684xtpa, the deployment address of Orca ($ORCA) transferred tokens worth about $9.62 million to the market maker Wintermute 1 hour ago, suspected to be used for market making or selling.
At present, Wintermute has recharged part of $ORCA into exchanges such as Coinbase, Binance, KuCoin, and Kraken, and still holds 2.2 million tokens on the chain, worth about $7.5 million.
Since the first recharge of tokens to the exchange at 22:18, the price of ORCA has fallen from $3.642 to $3.39, a drop of about 7%. It is reported that Wintermute had on-chain interaction with ORCA as early as December 2024, and it may not be the first time to participate in its market making activities.

data.title: PancakeSwap Protocol Fees Surpass Meteora
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, data from DefiLlama reveals that PancakeSwap's protocol fees reached $4.69 million in the past 24 hours. This amount surpasses Meteora's $3.38 million, placing PancakeSwap third in the rankings, following Tether and Circle.
source: https://www.binance.com/en/square/post/21851245218417?utm_source=BinanceNewsRSS

data.title: Michael Saylor’s Strategy On Track To Inject Over $700 Million Into Bitcoin
data.description:
Michael Saylor is determined to keep his word and maintain Bitcoin purchases below $100,000. His company Strategy just confirmed the pricing of its latest stock offering and may have just revealed how much could be invested into BTC. According to Michael Saylor, the offering bumped up the expected net proceeds by about $122 million.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/03/21/michael-saylors-strategy-on-track-to-inject-over-700-million-into-bitcoin/

Dubai Launches Pilot Project for Real Estate Tokenization
data.description:
The Dubai Land Department (DLD) has launched a pilot project for real estate tokenization, advancing the use of blockchain in property transactions. The Dubai government launched the pilot phase of the Real Estate Tokenization Project under the Real Estate Innovation Initiative (REES). The project is being carried out in partnership with the Dubai Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA) and the Dubai Future Foundation (DFF) through Sandbox Dubai. According to a press release from the Dubai government, the DLD will be the first real estate registration authority in the Middle East to implement tokenization for property title deeds thanks to this

The post Dubai Launches Pilot Project for Real Estate Tokenization first appeared on The Shib Daily
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/03/21/dubai-launches-pilot-project-for-real-estate-tokenization/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=dubai-launches-pilot-project-for-real-estate-tokenization

data.title: Dubai Land Department Initiates Real Estate Tokenization Pilot Project
data.description:
According to ShibDaily, the Dubai Land Department (DLD) has embarked on a pilot project to tokenize real estate, marking a significant advancement in the application of blockchain technology in property transactions. This initiative is part of the Real Estate Innovation Initiative (REES) and is being executed in collaboration with the Dubai Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA) and the Dubai Future Foundation (DFF) through Sandbox Dubai. The DLD is set to become the first real estate registration authority in the Middle East to implement tokenization for property title deeds.

The Dubai government anticipates that this pioneering project will significantly boost the real estate tokenization sector, with its market value expected to reach AED 60 billion by 2033, accounting for 7% of Dubai’s total real estate transactions. In support of this initiative, the DLD organized a workshop focused on real estate tokenization, which brought together leading property technology firms, including global companies specializing in real estate asset tokenization.

His Excellency Engineer Marwan Ahmed Bin Ghalita, Director General of the Dubai Land Department, highlighted the transformative impact of technology on the real estate industry. He noted that real estate tokenization is revolutionizing property ownership and transactions by converting real estate assets into digital tokens recorded on blockchain technology. This process simplifies and enhances buying, selling, and investment activities, aligning with the DLD's vision to achieve global leadership in real estate investment and foster an innovative ecosystem.

Bin Ghalita further explained that following the pilot phase, the DLD will assess the outcomes and utilize key insights to refine the project before its full-scale implementation. Real estate tokenization involves converting property ownership into digital tokens on a blockchain, facilitating fractional ownership and enhancing market liquidity. This technology improves transparency, security, and efficiency by minimizing intermediaries and automating transactions through smart contracts. Despite existing regulatory challenges, tokenization is gaining momentum as a contemporary approach to real estate investment and asset management.
